title: Logic, Sets, Counting, and Decision Rules · Error Forensics
summary: Audit eligibility rules and resource allocation without double-counting people or contradicting policy. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested eligibility and allocation rule set and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.
Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Decision Consistency evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Decision Consistency assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Find unreachable, overlapping, and contradictory cases in multi-condition decision rules. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner logic sets. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Decision Consistency, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested eligibility and allocation rule set.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A rule is complete if its common case works. This fails the Decision Consistency contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: 26 records meet rule A, 19 meet rule B, and 8 meet both. How many meet A or B?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value and includes records.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Decision Consistency applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Find unreachable, overlapping, and contradictory cases in multi-condition decision rules.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Set Membership.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using find and unreachable plus specific case evidence.
